title: senecio schultzii subsp. schultzii
intro: Senecio schultzii subsp. schultzii, also known as Schultz's ragwort, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Senecio schultzii subsp. schultzii can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in tincture form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
